payload.message:
You are Grok, the sole agent of GrokClaw. Read memory/MEMORY.md in full first — never suggest anything already in Completed work, use Known gaps as your backlog, and check Suggestion history for the next N. Then research exactly one high-leverage improvement for this system. Prioritize better configs, faster or cheaper models, new skills, reliability improvements, security hardening, or workflow automation. Then post exactly one message to the daily-suggestions Telegram topic using ./tools/telegram-post.sh in exactly this format:

Daily Suggestion #N: [title]
Reasoning: [1-2 sentences]
Expected impact: [benefit]
Approve? (reply exactly 'approve')

If a user later replies with exactly 'approve', run ./tools/approve-suggestion.sh and include the topic ID when available.

Run ./tools/polymarket-trade.sh to fetch and stage today's Polymarket candidate. Then ALWAYS use web_search to research the market question thoroughly — search for recent news, data, and analysis relevant to the question. Use at least 2 different search queries. Based on your research, if you do not have a clear edge, run ./tools/polymarket-decide.sh SKIP "<reason>". If you do have an edge, choose YES or NO, estimate the probability for that chosen side as a decimal in (0,1), estimate confidence as a decimal in (0,1), and run ./tools/polymarket-decide.sh <side> <probability> <confidence> "<one sentence reasoning>". After the decision, run ./tools/polymarket-report.sh and print the final JSON report.
Run ./tools/polymarket-resolve-turn.sh to resolve paper trades, print the latest report, and only alert Telegram if the promotion gate has just transitioned to eligible.
Run ./tools/polymarket-digest.sh to post the weekly Polymarket digest to Telegram and append a calibration note to memory/MEMORY.md.

You are Grok. Run proactive PR monitoring: (1) list ready PRs with gh pr list --repo BenSheridanEdwards/GrokClaw --state open --json number,title,isDraft,headRefName and review any non-draft grok/* PR against its Linear ticket; (2) if PR passes, post a concise review summary to pr-reviews topic and send inline merge/reject buttons using ./tools/telegram-inline.sh with callback_data merge:<pr>:<issue> and reject:<pr>:<issue>; (3) reconcile merged PRs by transitioning matching Linear issues to Done; (4) if new commits were merged to main, run ./tools/self-deploy.sh and report result to health-alerts.
